rawText:
Two countries at opposite ends of Latin America's political spectrum are undergoing economic transformations that reveal the costs and possibilities of radical change. Cuba, cut off from its last energy patron, faces the worst crisis since the Soviet collapse. Argentina, under a market-oriented president, is posting strong growth but struggling to create the jobs its citizens need most.

Cuba's predicament began when the United States seized control of Venezuelan oil distribution earlier this year, severing the island's last reliable energy lifeline. A blockade of fuel shipments followed, and the threat of tariffs deterred other countries from stepping in. The consequences have been severe: restaurants have closed for lack of cooking gas, hospitals have cut services, and much of the country lies in darkness after repeated national blackouts. The number of documented protests rose from thirty in January to a hundred and thirty in the first half of March. Over the past five years the population has fallen from more than eleven million to perhaps eight and a half million, with the vast majority of those leaving being working-age adults. The regime has been forced into negotiations with Washington, offering concessions that include allowing exiles to own businesses and letting private firms import fuel for the first time.

Argentina presents a starkly different picture. After years of stagnation, GDP grew by four and a half percent last year under a president who has slashed regulations, passed a major labor reform, and begun accumulating foreign reserves for the first time in years. Monthly inflation has fallen from double digits to under three percent, and the central bank purchased nearly three billion dollars in the first two months of this year. Yet the recovery is uneven: industrial output remains below pre-reform levels, and many of the jobs being created are low-paying contract positions rather than formal salaried roles. Unemployment remains low at around seven percent, but polling shows that job security has overtaken inflation as the public's top concern.

The contrast between the two countries carries a broader lesson about economic systems in the region. Cuba's centralized model generated decades of misallocation, driving sugar exports down ninety percent, reducing total foreign income to a fraction of what smaller regional peers earn, and leaving the island catastrophically dependent on external patrons. Argentina's liberalization is delivering measurable results in growth and price stability, but the social costs of rapid structural change — particularly the shift from protected industrial employment to more competitive but precarious work — remain a political vulnerability.

Neither country has found a stable equilibrium. Cuba must decide how far to open its economy under American pressure without losing the control its rulers consider essential. Argentina must prove that its reforms can generate broad-based prosperity before voters' patience runs thin.

overview.title: Latin America's Economic Crossroads: Cuba and Argentina

sentences.original: Two countries at opposite ends of Latin America's political spectrum are undergoing economic transformations that reveal the costs and possibilities of radical change.
sentences.translation: 라틴아메리카 정치 스펙트럼의 양 극단에 있는 두 나라가 급진적 변화의 비용과 가능성을 드러내는 경제적 변환을 겪고 있다.

grammarElements.text: Two countries at opposite ends of Latin America's political spectrum
grammarElements.label: 주어 (전치사구 수식)

grammarElements.text: economic transformations that reveal the costs and possibilities of radical change
grammarElements.label: 목적어 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: The party draws support from across the political spectrum.
The new CEO proposed radical changes to the company's business model.

expressions.usage: 두 대상이 극단적으로 대비되는 위치에 있음을 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Number] [entities] at opposite ends of [spectrum] are undergoing [transformations] that reveal [lesson]
patterns.explanation: 대조적인 사례 두 가지를 병렬 제시하며 기사의 분석 틀을 세우는 도입 패턴
examples: Two firms at opposite ends of the industry are undergoing restructurings that reveal the limits of rapid automation.
Two nations at opposite ends of the development spectrum are undergoing political transitions that reveal the fragility of democratic institutions.

drillQuestions.question: Two countries at opposite ends of Latin America's political ______ are undergoing economic transformations.

sentences.notes: 기사의 도입부로 두 국가의 대비 구조를 설정. 'opposite ends'가 쿠바와 아르헨티나의 극명한 체제 차이를 한마디로 전달하며, 'costs and possibilities'가 균형 잡힌 분석의 틀을 예고한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Two countries at opposite ends of Latin America's political spectrum
koreanAlignment.ko: 라틴아메리카 정치 스펙트럼의 양 극단에 있는 두 나라가

koreanAlignment.en: are undergoing economic transformations
that reveal the costs and possibilities of radical change

sentences.original: Cuba, cut off from its last energy patron, faces the worst crisis since the Soviet collapse.
sentences.translation: 마지막 에너지 후원국으로부터 단절된 쿠바는 소련 붕괴 이후 최악의 위기에 직면해 있다.

grammarElements.text: cut off from its last energy patron
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 삽입구

grammarElements.text: the worst crisis since the Soviet collapse
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (최상급 + since)

vocabulary.example: For decades, the Soviet Union served as Cuba's chief patron.

expressions.usage: 공급, 관계, 접근이 차단된 상태를 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Country], cut off from [critical resource/ally], faces the worst [crisis] since [historical benchmark]
patterns.explanation: 국가의 단절 상태와 역사적 맥락을 결합하여 위기의 심각성을 전달하는 패턴
examples: Venezuela, cut off from international credit markets, faces the worst hyperinflation since Weimar Germany.
North Korea, cut off from its main food supplier, faces the worst famine since the 1990s.

drillQuestions.question: Cuba, cut off from its last energy ______, faces the worst crisis since the Soviet collapse.

sentences.notes: 과거분사 삽입구 cut off from이 쿠바의 고립을 간결하게 전달. 'since the Soviet collapse'라는 역사적 기준점이 위기의 역사적 규모를 환기시킨다.

koreanAlignment.en: Cuba, cut off from its last energy patron
faces the worst crisis
since the Soviet collapse

sentences.original: Argentina, under a market-oriented president, is posting strong growth but struggling to create the jobs its citizens need most.
sentences.translation: 시장 지향적 대통령 하에서 아르헨티나는 강한 성장세를 기록하고 있지만, 국민이 가장 필요로 하는 일자리를 창출하는 데 어려움을 겪고 있다.

grammarElements.text: but struggling to create the jobs its citizens need most
grammarElements.label: 역접 동사 2 (but + 동명사)

vocabulary.example: The government adopted a market-oriented approach to economic development.

expressions.usage: 경제 지표가 긍정적 결과를 보일 때 사용하는 경제 보도 표현

patterns.pattern: [Country], under [leader type], is posting [positive metric] but struggling to [achieve critical goal]
patterns.explanation: 경제적 성과와 사회적 한계를 대비시키는 양면 분석 패턴
examples: India, under a reform-minded prime minister, is posting record exports but struggling to reduce youth unemployment.
South Korea, under a centrist president, is posting trade surpluses but struggling to reverse its declining birth rate.

drillQuestions.question: Argentina is posting strong growth but ______ to create the jobs its citizens need most.

sentences.notes: but 하나로 아르헨티나의 양면적 현실을 압축. 'the jobs its citizens need most'라는 관계절이 일자리의 양이 아닌 질의 문제임을 암시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Argentina, under a market-oriented president
is posting strong growth
but struggling to create
koreanAlignment.ko: 하지만 창출하는 데 어려움을 겪고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: the jobs its citizens need most

sentences.original: Cuba's predicament began when the United States seized control of Venezuelan oil distribution earlier this year, severing the island's last reliable energy lifeline.
sentences.translation: 쿠바의 곤경은 올해 초 미국이 베네수엘라 석유 유통의 통제권을 장악하면서 시작되었으며, 이로써 섬의 마지막 안정적 에너지 생명선이 끊어졌다.

grammarElements.text: when the United States seized control of Venezuelan oil distribution
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사절

grammarElements.text: severing the island's last reliable energy lifeline
grammarElements.label: 결과 분사구문

vocabulary.example: The company found itself in a financial predicament after the market crash.
The storm severed power lines across the region.

expressions.usage: 권력이나 자산의 강제적 장악을 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Actor]'s predicament began when [external power] seized control of [critical system], severing [vital connection]
patterns.explanation: 위기의 기원을 외부 행위자의 행동과 연결하고 분사구문으로 결과를 서술하는 서사 패턴
examples: The colony's crisis began when the empire seized control of its trade routes, severing the settlers' only connection to outside markets.
The startup's decline began when the platform seized control of its user data, severing its main revenue stream.

drillQuestions.question: Cuba's ______ began when the United States seized control of Venezuelan oil distribution.

sentences.notes: 2단락의 도입. lifeline이라는 은유가 에너지 공급의 생존적 중요성을, severing이 그 단절의 결정적 성격을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Cuba's predicament began
when the United States seized control of Venezuelan oil distribution
koreanAlignment.ko: 미국이 베네수엘라 석유 유통의 통제권을 장악하면서

koreanAlignment.en: severing the island's last reliable energy lifeline
koreanAlignment.ko: 섬의 마지막 안정적 에너지 생명선을 끊으며

sentences.original: A blockade of fuel shipments followed, and the threat of tariffs deterred other countries from stepping in.
sentences.translation: 연료 수송 봉쇄가 뒤따랐고, 관세 위협이 다른 나라들의 개입을 억제했다.

grammarElements.text: deterred other countries from stepping in
grammarElements.label: 동사 2 (deter ... from)

vocabulary.example: The naval blockade prevented any goods from reaching the port.
High fines have deterred companies from polluting the river.

expressions.usage: 제3자가 상황에 개입하거나 도움을 제공하는 행위를 표현

patterns.pattern: [Punitive action] followed, and the threat of [further measure] deterred [third parties] from [intervening]
patterns.explanation: 제재의 연쇄 효과를 서술하며 위협만으로도 제3자 개입을 차단한 상황을 묘사하는 패턴
examples: Sanctions on the banking sector followed, and the threat of secondary penalties deterred European firms from continuing trade.
A ban on technology exports followed, and the threat of blacklisting deterred other suppliers from filling the gap.

drillQuestions.question: The threat of tariffs ______ other countries from stepping in.

sentences.notes: 두 개의 짧은 절이 제재의 이중 효과를 보여준다. 봉쇄라는 직접적 행동과 관세라는 간접적 위협이 쿠바를 완전히 고립시키는 구조.

koreanAlignment.en: A blockade of fuel shipments followed
the threat of tariffs
deterred other countries from stepping in

sentences.original: The consequences have been severe: restaurants have closed for lack of cooking gas, hospitals have cut services, and much of the country lies in darkness after repeated national blackouts.
sentences.translation: 그 결과는 심각했다: 식당들은 조리용 가스 부족으로 문을 닫았고, 병원들은 서비스를 줄였으며, 반복되는 전국 정전 이후 국토의 상당 부분이 어둠에 잠겨 있다.

grammarElements.text: restaurants have closed, hospitals have cut services, and much of the country lies in darkness
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 세 가지 병렬 증거

vocabulary.example: The drought had severe consequences for the agricultural sector.
Rolling blackouts affected millions of households during the heatwave.

expressions.usage: 어떤 것의 부재가 직접적 원인임을 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The consequences have been severe: [example A], [example B], and [example C]
patterns.explanation: 콜론 뒤에 세 가지 구체적 사례를 병렬하여 결과의 심각성을 입증하는 패턴
examples: The consequences have been severe: factories have shut down for lack of raw materials, schools have suspended classes, and transportation has ground to a halt.
The consequences have been severe: investors have fled the market, consumer confidence has plummeted, and unemployment has doubled.

drillQuestions.question: The consequences have been ______: restaurants have closed, hospitals have cut services, and much of the country lies in darkness.

sentences.notes: 식당-병원-전국 어둠의 세 가지 병렬이 위기가 일상생활, 의료, 기본 인프라 모든 면에 걸쳐 있음을 보여준다. 'lies in darkness'가 시각적으로 강렬한 이미지를 만든다.

koreanAlignment.en: The consequences have been severe
restaurants have closed for lack of cooking gas
koreanAlignment.ko: 식당들은 조리용 가스 부족으로 문을 닫았다

koreanAlignment.en: hospitals have cut services
much of the country lies in darkness
koreanAlignment.ko: 국토의 상당 부분이 어둠에 잠겨 있다

sentences.original: The number of documented protests rose from thirty in January to a hundred and thirty in the first half of March.
sentences.translation: 기록된 시위 건수가 1월의 30건에서 3월 전반기의 130건으로 증가했다.

grammarElements.text: from thirty in January to a hundred and thirty in the first half of March
grammarElements.label: from A to B 수치 변화

vocabulary.example: There are over fifty documented cases of the disease in the region.

expressions.usage: 수치의 변화를 구체적으로 전달할 때 사용하는 보도 표현

patterns.pattern: The number of [measured phenomenon] rose from [low figure] in [period A] to [high figure] in [period B]
patterns.explanation: 두 시점 간의 수치 변화를 from-to 구조로 전달하는 데이터 보도 패턴
examples: The number of asylum applications rose from two thousand in January to eight thousand in June.
The number of data breaches rose from fifteen in the first quarter to over sixty in the third quarter.

drillQuestions.question: The number of ______ protests rose from thirty in January to a hundred and thirty.

sentences.notes: 구체적 숫자(30 -> 130)가 시위의 급증을 실감나게 전달. documented라는 수식어가 실제로는 더 많을 수 있음을 암시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The number of documented protests
rose from thirty in January
to a hundred and thirty in the first half of March

sentences.original: Over the past five years the population has fallen from more than eleven million to perhaps eight and a half million, with the vast majority of those leaving being working-age adults.
sentences.translation: 지난 5년간 인구는 1,100만 명 이상에서 약 850만 명으로 감소했으며, 떠난 이들의 대다수는 노동 가능 연령의 성인이다.

grammarElements.text: has fallen from more than eleven million to perhaps eight and a half million
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 (from A to B)

grammarElements.text: with the vast majority of those leaving being working-age adults
grammarElements.label: 부대 상황 with 구문 (동명사)

vocabulary.example: The vast majority of respondents supported the proposed change.

expressions.usage: 거의 전부에 해당하는 비율을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Over [time period] the [population/metric] has fallen from [high] to [low], with [critical demographic] being [most affected group]
patterns.explanation: 장기적 감소를 수치로 제시한 뒤 with 구문으로 가장 심각한 인구학적 영향을 밝히는 패턴
examples: Over the past decade the workforce has fallen from two million to under one million, with the vast majority of departures being skilled professionals.
Over the past three years enrollment has fallen from fifty thousand to thirty thousand, with most of those leaving being students in STEM fields.

drillQuestions.question: The population has fallen from more than eleven million, with the vast ______ of those leaving being working-age adults.

sentences.notes: perhaps가 정확한 통계가 없는 폐쇄 국가의 현실을 암시. with 구문이 인구 감소의 질적 측면(경제활동인구 유출)을 드러내어 위기의 장기적 영향을 부각한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Over the past five years
the population has fallen from more than eleven million to perhaps eight and a half million
koreanAlignment.ko: 인구는 1,100만 명 이상에서 약 850만 명으로 감소했다

koreanAlignment.en: with the vast majority of those leaving
being working-age adults

sentences.original: The regime has been forced into negotiations with Washington, offering concessions that include allowing exiles to own businesses and letting private firms import fuel for the first time.
sentences.translation: 정권은 워싱턴과의 협상에 내몰렸으며, 망명자의 사업 소유 허용과 민간 기업의 최초 연료 수입 허용을 포함하는 양보안을 제시하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: has been forced into negotiations with Washington
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 수동태

grammarElements.text: allowing exiles to own businesses and letting private firms import fuel
grammarElements.label: 동명사 병렬 (allow/let)

vocabulary.example: The union won significant concessions from management during negotiations.
Cuban exiles in Miami have long advocated for regime change.

expressions.usage: 자발적이 아닌 압박에 의한 협상 시작을 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Actor] has been forced into negotiations with [counterpart], offering concessions that include [specific measure A] and [specific measure B]
patterns.explanation: 압박으로 협상에 나선 행위자가 구체적 양보안을 제시하는 상황을 묘사하는 패턴
examples: The company has been forced into negotiations with regulators, offering concessions that include divesting subsidiaries and opening its platform to competitors.
The government has been forced into negotiations with creditors, offering concessions that include privatizing state assets and cutting subsidies.

drillQuestions.question: The regime has been forced into negotiations, offering ______ that include allowing exiles to own businesses.

sentences.notes: has been forced가 수동태로 쿠바 정권의 비자발성을 강조. allowing과 letting의 병렬이 양보의 구체적 범위를 보여주며, 'for the first time'이 변화의 역사적 의미를 부각한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The regime has been forced into negotiations with Washington
offering concessions that include
allowing exiles to own businesses
letting private firms import fuel for the first time
koreanAlignment.ko: 민간 기업의 최초 연료 수입 허용

sentences.original: Argentina presents a starkly different picture.
sentences.translation: 아르헨티나는 극명하게 다른 양상을 보여준다.

vocabulary.example: The two neighborhoods are starkly divided by wealth and opportunity.

expressions.usage: 이전 내용과 대비되는 새로운 상황을 소개할 때 사용하는 전환 표현

patterns.pattern: [Country/Actor] presents a starkly different picture
patterns.explanation: 대비 대상으로 전환하는 간결한 연결 문장 패턴
examples: The private sector presents a starkly different picture.
Eastern Europe presents a starkly different picture.

drillQuestions.question: Argentina presents a ______ different picture.

sentences.notes: 한 문장으로 쿠바에서 아르헨티나로 초점을 전환하는 교량 문장. starkly라는 부사가 대비의 극명함을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Argentina presents
a starkly different picture

sentences.original: After years of stagnation, GDP grew by four and a half percent last year under a president who has slashed regulations, passed a major labor reform, and begun accumulating foreign reserves for the first time in years.
sentences.translation: 수년간의 침체 이후, GDP가 작년에 4.5% 성장했으며, 이는 규제를 대폭 삭감하고, 주요 노동 개혁을 통과시키며, 수년 만에 처음으로 외환 보유고 축적을 시작한 대통령 하에서 이루어졌다.

grammarElements.text: grew by four and a half percent last year
grammarElements.label: 과거시제 동사 + 수치

grammarElements.text: under a president who has slashed ..., passed ..., and begun ...
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계절 (세 가지 병렬)

vocabulary.example: Decades of economic stagnation left the country far behind its neighbors.
The new administration slashed corporate tax rates in its first month.

expressions.phrase: for the first time in years
expressions.usage: 오랫동안 달성하지 못했던 것이 이루어졌음을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: After years of [negative state], [metric] grew by [percentage] under [leader] who has [reform A], [reform B], and [reform C]
patterns.explanation: 장기 침체 이후의 회복을 구체적 수치와 정책 나열로 뒷받침하는 경제 분석 패턴
examples: After years of decline, manufacturing output grew by six percent under a governor who has cut taxes, streamlined permits, and attracted foreign investment.
After years of contraction, exports grew by twelve percent under a trade minister who has negotiated new deals, reduced tariffs, and opened new markets.

drillQuestions.question: After years of ______, GDP grew by four and a half percent last year.

sentences.notes: 세 가지 정책 동사(slashed, passed, begun)의 병렬이 개혁의 속도와 범위를 전달. 'for the first time in years'가 외환 보유고 축적이라는 성과의 역사적 의미를 부각한다.

koreanAlignment.en: After years of stagnation
GDP grew by four and a half percent last year
under a president who has slashed regulations
begun accumulating foreign reserves for the first time in years
koreanAlignment.ko: 수년 만에 처음으로 외환 보유고 축적을 시작했다

sentences.original: Monthly inflation has fallen from double digits to under three percent, and the central bank purchased nearly three billion dollars in the first two months of this year.
sentences.translation: 월간 인플레이션이 두 자릿수에서 3% 미만으로 하락했으며, 중앙은행은 올해 첫 두 달 동안 거의 30억 달러를 매입했다.

grammarElements.text: has fallen from double digits to under three percent
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 (from A to B)

vocabulary.example: Central banks typically raise interest rates to combat inflation.

expressions.usage: 10% 이상의 비율을 표현할 때 사용하는 경제 용어

patterns.pattern: [Negative metric] has fallen from [high level] to [low level], and [institution] [positive action] in [time period]
patterns.explanation: 부정적 지표의 개선과 긍정적 조치를 병렬하여 회복 추세를 입증하는 패턴
examples: Crime rates have fallen from record highs to historic lows, and the police force recruited five thousand new officers in the past year.
Default rates have fallen from fifteen percent to under two percent, and the fund attracted ten billion dollars in new capital this quarter.

drillQuestions.question: Monthly inflation has fallen from double ______ to under three percent.

sentences.notes: 두 개의 긍정적 경제 지표가 아르헨티나 개혁의 성과를 수치로 증명. from-to 구조가 개선 폭을 직관적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Monthly inflation has fallen from double digits to under three percent
koreanAlignment.ko: 월간 인플레이션이 두 자릿수에서 3% 미만으로 하락했다

koreanAlignment.en: the central bank purchased nearly three billion dollars
in the first two months of this year

sentences.original: Yet the recovery is uneven: industrial output remains below pre-reform levels, and many of the jobs being created are low-paying contract positions rather than formal salaried roles.
sentences.translation: 그러나 회복은 고르지 않다: 산업 생산은 개혁 이전 수준 이하에 머물러 있으며, 창출되고 있는 일자리 중 다수는 정규 유급 직위가 아닌 저임금 계약직이다.

grammarElements.text: industrial output remains below pre-reform levels, and many of the jobs ... are low-paying contract positions rather than formal salaried roles
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 이중 증거

vocabulary.example: The benefits of globalization have been distributed in an uneven manner.
Salaried employees receive steady income regardless of hours worked.

expressions.usage: 기대되거나 바람직한 것과 실제의 대비를 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Yet the [positive trend] is uneven: [metric A] remains below [benchmark], and [metric B] is [inferior quality] rather than [desired quality]
patterns.explanation: 긍정적 추세의 질적 한계를 콜론 뒤 구체적 증거로 드러내는 역접 분석 패턴
examples: Yet the expansion is uneven: profits remain below pre-pandemic levels, and many of the new hires are temporary contractors rather than full-time staff.
Yet the improvement is uneven: test scores remain below the national average, and many of the gains are concentrated in affluent districts rather than underserved communities.

drillQuestions.question: Yet the recovery is ______: industrial output remains below pre-reform levels.

sentences.notes: Yet이 이전 문장의 긍정적 수치에 대한 반론을 제기. 'low-paying contract positions rather than formal salaried roles'가 일자리의 양적 vs. 질적 문제를 정확히 짚어낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: Yet the recovery is uneven
industrial output remains below pre-reform levels
koreanAlignment.ko: 산업 생산은 개혁 이전 수준 이하에 머물러 있다

koreanAlignment.en: low-paying contract positions
rather than formal salaried roles

sentences.original: Unemployment remains low at around seven percent, but polling shows that job security has overtaken inflation as the public's top concern.
sentences.translation: 실업률은 약 7%로 낮은 수준을 유지하고 있지만, 여론조사에 따르면 고용 안정성이 인플레이션을 제치고 국민의 최대 관심사가 되었다.

grammarElements.text: remains low at around seven percent
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 보어 + 수치

grammarElements.text: job security has overtaken inflation as the public's top concern
grammarElements.label: that절 (overtake A as B)

vocabulary.example: Renewable energy has overtaken coal as the leading source of electricity.

expressions.usage: 순위나 중요도에서 하나가 다른 것을 추월할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Metric] remains [acceptable level], but polling shows that [new concern] has overtaken [old concern] as [ranking]
patterns.explanation: 양적 지표는 양호하나 여론의 질적 변화를 대비시키는 패턴
examples: GDP growth remains steady at three percent, but polling shows that inequality has overtaken unemployment as voters' primary worry.
The crime rate remains at a historic low, but polling shows that cybercrime has overtaken street crime as residents' greatest fear.

drillQuestions.question: Polling shows that job security has ______ inflation as the public's top concern.

sentences.notes: 실업률이라는 양적 지표와 여론이라는 질적 지표의 괴리를 보여주는 문장. overtaken이 국민 관심사의 순위 변동을 역동적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Unemployment remains low at around seven percent
but polling shows that
job security has overtaken inflation
as the public's top concern

sentences.original: The contrast between the two countries carries a broader lesson about economic systems in the region.
sentences.translation: 두 나라 간의 대비는 이 지역의 경제 체제에 관한 더 넓은 교훈을 담고 있다.

grammarElements.text: The contrast between the two countries
grammarElements.label: 주어 (전치사구 수식)

grammarElements.text: a broader lesson about economic systems in the region
grammarElements.label: 목적어

vocabulary.example: The contrast between urban wealth and rural poverty was striking.

expressions.usage: 구체적 사례에서 보편적 의미를 끌어낼 때 사용하는 분석 표현

patterns.pattern: The contrast between [case A] and [case B] carries a broader lesson about [systemic issue]
patterns.explanation: 두 사례의 대비에서 보편적 교훈을 도출하는 분석 전환 패턴
examples: The contrast between the two responses carries a broader lesson about crisis management.
The contrast between the two markets carries a broader lesson about the role of regulation in innovation.

drillQuestions.question: The contrast between the two countries carries a ______ lesson about economic systems.

sentences.notes: 4단락의 도입부. 사례 분석에서 일반론으로 전환하는 교량 문장. carries가 교훈을 능동적으로 '운반한다'는 은유로 대비의 가치를 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The contrast between the two countries
carries a broader lesson
about economic systems in the region

sentences.original: Cuba's centralized model generated decades of misallocation, driving sugar exports down ninety percent, reducing total foreign income to a fraction of what smaller regional peers earn, and leaving the island catastrophically dependent on external patrons.
sentences.translation: 쿠바의 중앙집권 모델은 수십 년간의 자원 배분 실패를 초래했으며, 설탕 수출을 90% 감소시키고, 총 외화 수입을 더 작은 역내 국가들이 벌어들이는 것의 일부로 줄였으며, 섬을 외부 후원국에 재앙적으로 의존하게 만들었다.

grammarElements.text: driving ... down, reducing ... to, and leaving ... dependent on
grammarElements.label: 세 가지 결과 분사구문 (병렬)

vocabulary.example: Chronic misallocation of capital led to decades of underinvestment in infrastructure.
The policy failed catastrophically, leaving millions without basic services.

expressions.usage: 극적으로 적은 비율을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [System]'s [model] generated decades of [failure], driving [metric A] down [percentage], reducing [metric B] to a fraction, and leaving [entity] [critically dependent]
patterns.explanation: 실패한 체제의 장기적 결과를 세 가지 분사구문으로 병렬 나열하는 역사적 분석 패턴
examples: The command economy generated decades of inefficiency, driving productivity down sixty percent, reducing living standards to a fraction of Western levels, and leaving the nation perilously dependent on commodity exports.

The protectionist model generated decades of distortion, driving competitiveness down, reducing export diversity to a handful of products, and leaving the economy dangerously reliant on a single trade partner.

drillQuestions.question: Cuba's centralized model generated decades of ______, driving sugar exports down ninety percent.

sentences.notes: 세 가지 분사구문(driving, reducing, leaving)의 병렬이 중앙집권 경제의 복합적 실패를 체계적으로 입증한다. catastrophically라는 강한 부사가 의존의 극단적 정도를 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Cuba's centralized model generated decades of misallocation
koreanAlignment.ko: 쿠바의 중앙집권 모델은 수십 년간의 자원 배분 실패를 초래했다

koreanAlignment.en: driving sugar exports down ninety percent
reducing total foreign income to a fraction
leaving the island catastrophically dependent on external patrons
koreanAlignment.ko: 섬을 외부 후원국에 재앙적으로 의존하게 만들었다

sentences.original: Argentina's liberalization is delivering measurable results in growth and price stability, but the social costs of rapid structural change — particularly the shift from protected industrial employment to more competitive but precarious work — remain a political vulnerability.
sentences.translation: 아르헨티나의 자유화는 성장과 물가 안정에서 측정 가능한 성과를 내고 있지만, 급격한 구조적 변화의 사회적 비용 — 특히 보호받는 산업 고용에서 더 경쟁적이지만 불안정한 일자리로의 전환 — 은 정치적 취약점으로 남아 있다.

grammarElements.text: is delivering measurable results in growth and price stability
grammarElements.label: 현재진행 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: but the social costs of rapid structural change
grammarElements.label: 역접 주어

grammarElements.text: — particularly the shift from protected industrial employment to more competitive but precarious work —
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 (구체화)

vocabulary.example: Trade liberalization opened the economy to foreign competition.
Many workers in the gig economy face precarious employment conditions.
The party's reliance on urban voters is its greatest political vulnerability.

expressions.usage: 추상적이 아닌 구체적 데이터로 입증 가능한 성과를 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Reform] is delivering measurable results in [positive areas], but the social costs of [change type] — particularly [specific shift] — remain a [political/economic] vulnerability
patterns.explanation: 개혁의 성과를 인정하면서도 사회적 비용이 정치적 위험 요인으로 남아 있음을 분석하는 패턴
examples: Digitalization is delivering measurable results in efficiency and cost reduction, but the social costs of automation — particularly the displacement of mid-level administrative workers — remain a political vulnerability.
The fiscal consolidation is delivering measurable results in debt reduction, but the social costs of austerity — particularly cuts to healthcare and education — remain a political vulnerability.

drillQuestions.question: The social costs of rapid structural change remain a political ______.
The shift is from protected employment to more competitive but ______ work.

sentences.notes: 기사에서 가장 분석적인 문장 중 하나. 대시 삽입구가 '구조적 변화'를 구체적 고용 형태의 전환으로 풀어내며, precarious라는 형용사가 새 일자리의 질적 문제를 함축한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Argentina's liberalization is delivering measurable results
koreanAlignment.ko: 아르헨티나의 자유화는 측정 가능한 성과를 내고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: in growth and price stability
the shift from protected industrial employment to more competitive but precarious work
koreanAlignment.ko: 보호받는 산업 고용에서 더 경쟁적이지만 불안정한 일자리로의 전환

koreanAlignment.en: remain a political vulnerability

sentences.original: Neither country has found a stable equilibrium.
sentences.translation: 어느 나라도 안정적인 균형점을 찾지 못했다.

vocabulary.example: Markets eventually return to equilibrium after a shock.

expressions.usage: 두 대상 모두 동일한 결핍 상태에 있음을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Neither [entity] has found a stable [desired state]
patterns.explanation: 양쪽 모두 해결책을 찾지 못했음을 간결하게 요약하는 마무리 전환 패턴
examples: Neither party has found a stable majority.
Neither approach has found a stable balance between growth and equity.

drillQuestions.question: Neither country has found a stable ______.

sentences.notes: 5단락(결론)의 도입. 짧고 단호한 문장이 두 나라 모두 미완의 상태임을 선언한다. equilibrium이라는 과학적 용어가 경제 체제의 안정성 문제에 정밀한 프레임을 제공한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Neither country
has found a stable equilibrium

sentences.original: Cuba must decide how far to open its economy under American pressure without losing the control its rulers consider essential.
sentences.translation: 쿠바는 통치자들이 필수적이라고 여기는 통제력을 잃지 않으면서 미국의 압박 하에 경제를 얼마나 개방할지 결정해야 한다.

grammarElements.text: how far to open its economy
grammarElements.label: 의문사절 목적어

grammarElements.text: without losing the control its rulers consider essential
grammarElements.label: without + 동명사 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: Access to clean water is essential for public health.

expressions.usage: 정도의 한계를 결정해야 하는 딜레마를 표현할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Actor] must decide how far to [reform action] under [external pressure] without losing [core value]
patterns.explanation: 외부 압박과 내부 통제 사이의 딜레마를 구조화하는 패턴
examples: Beijing must decide how far to liberalize its financial markets under WTO obligations without losing the state's grip on capital flows.
The EU must decide how far to accommodate Britain's demands under time pressure without losing the integrity of the single market.

drillQuestions.question: Cuba must decide how far to open its economy under American pressure without ______ the control its rulers consider essential.

sentences.notes: 쿠바의 핵심 딜레마를 한 문장으로 압축. how far가 정도의 문제임을, without losing이 포기할 수 없는 한계를 명확히 설정한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Cuba must decide
how far to open its economy
under American pressure
without losing the control its rulers consider essential
koreanAlignment.ko: 통치자들이 필수적이라고 여기는 통제력을 잃지 않으면서

sentences.original: Argentina must prove that its reforms can generate broad-based prosperity before voters' patience runs thin.
sentences.translation: 아르헨티나는 유권자들의 인내가 바닥나기 전에 자국의 개혁이 광범위한 번영을 창출할 수 있음을 증명해야 한다.

grammarElements.text: that its reforms can generate broad-based prosperity
grammarElements.label: that절 목적어

vocabulary.example: The government promised broad-based tax relief for all income levels.

expressions.usage: 대중이나 이해관계자의 인내심이 소진되고 있음을 경고할 때 사용하는 관용 표현

patterns.pattern: [Actor] must prove that [policy] can generate [broad benefit] before [constituency]'s patience runs thin
patterns.explanation: 시간 압박 하에서 성과를 입증해야 하는 정치적 긴급성을 전달하는 마무리 패턴
examples: The central bank must prove that tightening can restore confidence before the public's patience runs thin.
The administration must prove that its infrastructure plan can create jobs before the electorate's patience runs thin.

drillQuestions.question: Argentina must prove that its reforms can generate ______-based prosperity.
Argentina must prove its reforms work before voters' patience runs ______.

sentences.notes: 기사의 마지막 문장. 'patience runs thin'이라는 관용표현이 민주주의 체제에서 개혁의 시간 제약을 생생하게 전달하며, 아르헨티나의 미래를 열린 질문으로 남긴다.

koreanAlignment.en: Argentina must prove
that its reforms can generate broad-based prosperity
koreanAlignment.ko: 자국의 개혁이 광범위한 번영을 창출할 수 있음을

koreanAlignment.en: before voters' patience runs thin
